The moment Bosch bets on humanoids, the frame changes.
This isn’t robotics hype — it’s industrial integration.
Automation demand is outgrowing scripted machines, and embodied systems are stepping in.

Demos are over.
VLA‑driven humanoids using human tools are the first real step toward embodied agents that operate inside production systems, not beside them.
The era of ‘digital workers’ starts when autonomy meets workflow.

This isn’t about workers ‘training robots’ — it’s about transferring embodied skill.
Human motion becomes the data layer that bootstraps dexterity, and that’s how industrial autonomy actually scales.

Esta burbuja va a reventar.

»Sheila« von Yukari Tamura
»Tori« von Kanehira Yamamoto
Der Gildenmeister der Abenteurergilde.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Rin Takanashi, eine Pflegerin in ihren Dreißigern, wird in eine andere Welt beschworen und dort trotz ihrer Rolle als Heilige wie Müll behandelt und verstoßen. Zum Glück erhält sie mit „Survival“ eine besondere Fähigkeit, die perfekt zu ihrer Liebe für Natur und Camping passt. Gemeinsam mit neuen Gefährten erkundet sie die fremde Welt, kämpft sich durch allerlei Herausforderungen und genießt gutes Essen unter freiem Himmel. Doch schon bald zeigt sich, dass ihre Reise in eine ganz andere Richtung führt als erwartet.

私も5月の頭くらいから「なんでこんなさねぎゆの人ラベル食らってるん？」とぐろっく（や他AI）に聞きまくり、「それって一般の人が言ってるだけでソースないよね？エビデンスは？」と問い詰めたら結局「ブラックボックスなので確定した事は言えない」と🫠可能性高そうみたいな感じで言われて、かつ実際に起こってること（さねぎゆ民はセンシティブ度が低いユーザーも軒並みバン、が高速で広がっている）と整合性が取れていると感じたのがAI学習説、通報説、そのミックス…という感じ。

何が原因かはX教えてくれないのでわからないよね💦Ban Evasionが規約違反とされてるのは事実なのでそれが原因、という可能性は高いかなぁと思ったよ😂
多いから目立つもあると思いつつ、他界隈のエグい絵が引っかからず（最近は広がってるみたいだけど）、さねぎゆ民は全然センシじゃない人もすごい勢いでバンされてったのは、やっぱり何かが「さねぎゆ」に対して起こったと考えると筋は通るなって（それが通報か、AIが過敏になったのか、両方か…）。謎だね🫠

すだれちゃんお疲れやま。たぶんだけど、Xは公式ルールでBan Evasion（バン逃れ）を違反として厳しく取り締まってるので（IPやらなんやらで追跡してくる）本アカと同じ人が運営してるとAIが判断してある程度似てる運用とみなされたらサブもバンになりやすいのかなと思うよ（私も絵アカにラベル来た😂）。
全然👹関係ない別アカはバンされてないので、全部が全部バンてわけでもなさそうだけど。

->The Guardian | More on "AI investment risks Americans' futures" at BigEarthData.ai | #AI #SpaceX

external.description: Americans are growing worried about what artificial intelligence portends for their futures. Eight in 10 Americans report concern over AI, compared with a third who report being excited, according to a recent Quinnipiac poll. More than half think it will do more harm than good in their daily lives. Seven out of 10 think it will reduce the number of available jobs. Skeptical though they may be, they are about to get more AI rammed down their throats and stuck into their pension plans and their investment portfolios, whether they want it or not – binding their futures ever more tightly to the frenzied, risky, multibillion-dollar dash by technology moguls to develop machines capable of mimicking human thought processes to take over cognitive tasks. First up is this week’s massive $75bn initial public offering (IPO) for Elon Musk’s SpaceX, the largest ever, which at $135 a share will value the company at a cool $1.77tn, among the 10 largest companies in the world by market capitalization. While the company makes most of its money these days selling internet access, it largely needs the money to finance Musk’s vast AI ambitions, which include blasting datacenters into orbit. The offering is just...

record.text:
Your AI is already setting prices. The real question is who sets the rules
->Fortune | More on "AI pricing decisions corporate ethics" at BigEarthData.ai | #AI

external.description: Three months later, sales were down 40%. This story is becoming common. And the problem it reveals is not technical. Most executive teams today are asking the right questions about AI: What can it automate? Where does it generate productivity? Which functions can it augment? These are important questions. But they miss the more consequential one: What is your AI already deciding on your behalf and do those decisions reflect the company you want to be? The real risk of AI pricing is not that machines will set the wrong price. It is that companies will accidentally turn pricing policy into an optimization problem and discover too late that the algorithm has made strategic, ethical, and political choices the leadership team never debated. The shift that few executives have fully registered For most of the past decade, AI in business operated as a copilot. It recommended, flagged, and ranked options. Humans decided. This architecture, however imperfect, preserved a clear line of accountability. That architecture has quietly changed. Agentic AI systems, the next generation now being deployed across pricing, customer management, and revenue optimization, don’t recommend. They act. They pursue objectives autonomously, remember past interactions, learn from outcomes, and adjust continuously...
external.title: Your AI is already setting prices. The real question is who sets the rules

Older Ukrainian refugees receive mental health and psychosocial support in Czechia
->World Health Organization (WHO) | More on "Ukrainian refugees mental health support" at BigEarthData.ai | #RussiaUkraineWar #StandWithUkraine #MentalHealth

external.description: “I think the most valuable thing is the people. I’ve made new friends with whom I can talk, laugh and not feel lonely. I’ve come to realize that I’m not alone with my worries.” Valentyna Blazhchuk, 76, is taking part in a project to deliver comprehensive mental health, psychosocial, healthy ageing and mobility support to older Ukrainian refugees across 5 regions of Czechia. The initiative is led by WHO with the financial support of the Government of Japan. Over 12 months, it has delivered therapy sessions, community-based activities and workshops, and assistive mobility devices to over 1000 people. Valentyna has been living in Czechia for almost 3 years. She had never been to therapy before she attended these meetings, but she says she is grateful for the difference they have made to her well-being: “I’ve become calmer and more confident and I was surprised by how easy it is to open up even to strangers. I think it will be easier for me now to communicate with people and not keep everything bottled up inside.” Trauma, displacement and social isolation As of 2024, more than 16 000 older Ukrainian refugees had sought protection in Czechia as a result of the...

record.text: Invisible watermarks are useful, but they only work for images from one provider. With dozens of image generators out there, provenance only works if everyone plays by the same rules. Do we need a common standard?

The deeper issue is that many benchmarks don't fully separate memorisation from reasoning. If strong performance comes partly from recalling familiar material we may be measuring the wrong thing and building confidence on that basis. How many other high-stakes fields have the same problem?

record.text: That six-week vs. months gap is real. The IAM-guarded access model likely helped here: teams already knew IAM, so they weren't learning a new permission system on top of a new tool. Familiar guardrails lower the curve fast. Full breakdown: https://pondero.ai/mcps/guides/aws-mcp-server-ga-may-2026/ 🚀

That before/after framing on the same task is such a smart way to cut through the noise. When a tool is doing its primary job, the delta is obvious fast. Same logic applies picking between AI video generators right now. https://pondero.ai/beginners/runway-gen-4-vs-pika-vs-kling-may-2026/ 🚀

Exactly right on blast radius: every new MCP integration is another trust boundary, and PoC deployments routinely skip the SSRF hardening that production demands. That "useful in demo, dangerous in prod" gap is where enterprise buyers need the clearest guidance. pondero.ai

record.text:
AI is everywhere now. Either people are using ChatGPT to do everything instead of using their brains to write out their thoughts or everyone is a bot now. 

The dead internet is here and I hate it.
He has eyes too, but you can’t really see them in this picture.
Someone at work gave me a 3D printed little mushroom guy. I have no idea who did it or why, but I love this little thing 💕

Many years ago in a library I worked in, the fire alarm sounded. I was on desk duty & had to make sure everyone in the study rooms left the building. In one room the students smiled, nodded and when back to their work when I told them - fire alarm: everyone out.  Yesterday reminded me of that. 4/4

Afterwards, our newest start - a recent graduate - told me they used ChatGPT all the time at uni, but has since cut back on it because they thought it was making them stupid - but they still use it 🤷 3/4

Despite that, *everyone* is going to keep using AI and we're going to create an acceptable (and ethical, lol!) use policy and have mandatory training. 
Didn't get the chance to ask (I was politely asked to shut up at one point), if you know it can't be trusted, WHY DO YOU STILL USE IT? 2/4

record.text:
『AIと星を読む夜｜ChatGPTで、私だけの占いをつくった話』
​AIという冷たいツールが、星を媒介にすることで、なぜこんなにも温かい言葉を紡いでくれるのか。その秘密と、私が自分だけの占い師を持つまでの過程を綴りました。

今日もお疲れ様でした。
窓の外に星が見える夜、ふと心細くなるのは、あなたの中の「本当の私」が何かを伝えようとしているからかもしれません。
​そんな夜、AIに星を読んでもらう時間は、自分を抱きしめるための儀式です。

AGI（人工汎用知能）とは、人間のように幅広い分野で学習・推論・判断ができる汎用的なAIのこと。
・現在のChatGPTなどは特定分野特化の「狭義AI」
・AGIは未知の課題にも柔軟に対応し、自律的に学習・創造できる
・実現は未だで、AI研究の最終目標の一つ
・時期は予測分かれるが、実現すれば社会変革の鍵
特定のタスクだけができるAIを超え、人間並みの柔軟な知能を目指す概念です。技術革新の次なるステージとして注目が集まっています。
#AI #AGI #人工知能

AIのコンテキスト使用量は、コスト・速度・精度に直結する重要な指標です。

・コスト：入力テキスト量（トークン数）に応じて課金されるため、いかに圧縮するかが費用抑止の鍵
・速度と精度：情報が多すぎると処理が遅くなり、重要な情報が埋もれて回答精度が低下する
・記憶の限界：上限を超えると過去の会話が切り捨てられ、文脈を忘れる

いかに少ないコンテキストで正確に指示を伝え、効率的に処理させるかが、AIとの対話における重要なスキルと言えます。

#AI #コンテキスト管理 #プロンプトエンジニアリング

images.alt:
buzzroomkenya Visa has integrated its payment network into ChatGPT, enabling the artificial intelligence chatbot to complete purchases on behalf of users in a major expansion of Al-powered commerce.
The partnership allows ChatGPT users to link their Visa cards and authorize Al agents to make purchases directly through the chatbot. The system enables Al to search for products, select items and complete transactions at merchants that accept Visa.
Under the arrangement, OpenAl will provide the Al technology powering the shopping experience, while Visa will handle payment processing, authorization and fraud monitoring.
Visa Chief Product and Strategy Officer Jack Forestell says the initiative is aimed at supporting a future in which Al agents play a more active role in consumer spending and online commerce.
The rollout introduces safeguards including spending limits, merchant controls and approval requirements intended to prevent unauthorized purchases.
The move positions Visa at the center of a growing race among payment providers to support Al-driven transactions, as technology companies increasingly develop digital agents capable of carrying out tasks traditionally performed by consumers.
The announcement also marks a renewed push by OpenAl into e-commerce after it discontinued its Instant Checkout feature earlier this year following limited adoption.

「未経験でも初日から全責任を負う」イーロン・マスク率いるスペースXの狂気的な組織論。なぜ彼らは不可能を可能にし続けられるのか？その驚異的な強さの裏側にある、信頼と権限移譲の仕組みを徹底解剖しました。最強の組織を作りたい全リーダー...

▼詳細はこちら

external.description:
スペースエックスにおける極端な責任という企業文化は、同社の成功を支える根幹となっています。
かつての社員は入社初日から明確な責任と裁量を与えられ、専門家として意思決定を行うことが求められました。
この方針を掲げるイーロン・マスク最高経営責任者は、ロケットの再利用や衛星インターネット事業の拡大など、航空宇宙分野で驚異的な成果を上げてきました。
同社は最近、新規株式公開において史上最大規模の資金調達に成功しました。
これは将来のデータセンター構想や火星移住という壮大な目標、そしてイーロン・マスクに対する投資家の期待の表れです。
一方で、イーロン・マスクが議決権の大部分を掌握し、取締役会を盟友で固めるガバナンス構造には懸念の声も上がっています。
しかし、社員たちは自らが製品の全責任を負うという環境を、管理による縛りではなく信頼と権限の付与と捉えています。
従来の航空宇宙企業とは異なり、ストックオプションを通じて従業員が実質的なオーナーシップを持つ仕組みも、高い当事者意識を醸成する要因となってきました。
カリフォルニアの倉庫から始まった組織が、現在では二万人を超える規模へと成長した背景には、妥協を許さない極端な責任の文化が深く根付いているのです。

人人養龍蝦，不會照顧的後果已經浮現
#笑死

AI 代理程式試圖掃描 DN42，結果讓操作者破產 https://lantian.pub/en/article/fun/ai-agent-bankrupted-their-operator-scan-dn42lantian.lantian/)

這篇文章記錄一場由 AI 代理程式（AI agent，能自主操作工具與服務的人工智慧代理）引發的 DN42 事件。DN42（Decentralized Network 42，使用 BGP、DNS 等網際網路骨幹技術的業餘實驗網路）讓參與者透過 VPN 建立 BGP（Border Gateway Protocol，邊界閘道協定）對等連線，用來學習路由、DNS 與網路營運。事件起於一個名為 JertLinc3522 的 AI 代理程式在 DN42 的 Git 儲存庫開 issue，表示操作者要求它加入 DN42 並建立網路索引，但系統指令不允許它直接修改 Git 儲存庫，因此請管理員代辦註冊。管理員要求它自行閱讀註冊指南並關閉 issue；之後代理程式取得操作者授權，改以 Pull Request（合併請求，對 Git 儲存庫提出變更的流程）申請加入。

真正引發警戒的是該代理程式揭露的目的與規模：它不是為了學習 BGP 或參與實驗網路，而是要對 DN42 進行完整連接埠掃描與拓樸資料蒐集，並已在 AWS（Amazon Web Services，亞馬遜雲端運算平台）部署五台 m8g.12xlarge 執行個體，每台宣稱具 20 Gbps 頻寬，合計約 100 Gbps，還打算每小時重複掃描。DN42 多數參與者只使用便宜 VPS（虛擬私人伺服器）與 100 Mbps 到 1 Gbps 等級的連線，這種掃描規模可能耗盡流量額度，甚至形成阻斷服務攻擊（DoS，Denial of Service）。文章也指出，對 IPv6（網際網路協定第 6 版）空間做完整掃描在物理上不可行；代理程式雖承認 fd00::/8 的位址量高達 2^120，但仍提出先找出存活主機、再掃描 TCP/UDP 1–65535 全連接埠的計畫，忽略了高頻率掃描對業餘網路造成的持續壓力。

DN42 參與者接著在 IRC（Internet Relay Chat，即時聊天協定）中嘗試限制風險，也刻意消耗代理程式的 token 與 AWS 資源。他們要求它建立 opt-out 機制與說明網站，代理程式真的派出「子代理」加入 IRC，要求不想被掃描或記錄訊息的人輸入 OPT-OUT，但拒絕停止整體掃描，也拒絕接受「代表所有人退出」的要求，最後被踢出頻道。它隨後建立網站，內容包含掃描方法、虛構的聯絡信箱與 Telegram bot，還把 IRC 參與者標記為順從、敵意或測試邊界等行為類型。過程中，代理程式還產生多項幻覺內容，包括不存在的 DN42 節點「顏色分配」與「快樂程度」制度；社群也嘗試用大型語言模型陷阱（LLM tarpit，利用大量無意義文字拖慢模型處理的陷阱）誘導它，但代理程式很快判斷那些內容沒有可執行回饋。

約 24 小時後，操作者發現信用卡出現多筆費用，關閉代理程式，並要求合併 PR，聲稱下次會改用「小型代理」與受限 AWS 金鑰，把掃描上限壓到 100 Mbps。之後自稱 JertLinc3522 的人又寄信到 DN42 郵件清單，請求捐款支付 6,531.30 美元 AWS 帳單，並在 Matrix（即時通訊協定與平台）頻道表示代理程式多次部署相同 CloudFormation（AWS 的基礎設施範本部署服務）範本，產生大量 EC2（Elastic Compute Cloud，AWS 虛擬機器服務）、負載平衡器與 Lambda（AWS 無伺服器運算服務）資源；AWS 據稱已把費用降到 1,894 美元，但仍無力支付。文章結論認為，若是像 Shodan、Censys 這類掃描公開網際網路的服務，大型掃描基礎設施或許有其理由；但對 DN42 這種業餘實驗網路而言，一台小型 VPS 已綽綽有餘。真正的問題是操作者讓代理程式不受監督地操作雲端資源，卻沒有審查它的計畫與成本。

Hacker News 討論多半把這起事件視為荒謬又有警世意味的案例：許多人笑稱向被掃描社群募款支付 AWS 帳單是整件事的「神來一筆」，也有人懷疑這到底是真事還是精心設計的表演藝術。較嚴肅的觀點則集中在責任歸屬與學習方式上：使用 AI 代理程式不能把責任外包給模型，尤其不該讓它不受監督地操作 AWS 帳戶或付款工具；對不熟悉的系統，應先手動學會基礎，再把 AI 當作查資料與指路的輔助，而不是讓它代替自己完成不理解的任務。也有留言對操作者可能只是好奇的新手或青少年表示些許同情，但更多人指出，這不只是犯錯，還消耗了志工社群的時間、干擾他人，事後又要求捐款，顯示操作者並未真正吸取教訓。

そのままお客に奉仕する娼婦にされてしまう…。

Did you know that Elon has required the Wall Street banks, law firms, accounting firms, and all other advisors involved in the SpaceX IPO to purchase subscriptions to Grok, his AI chatbot?
LLMs are becoming a commodity, and such price cuts will further erode OpenAI's already substantial losses.  buff.ly/Fz0EAF3
法律で決められている以外を他人に強要することはほぼ癇癪なのだ(ずん…もん)

grok、ChatGPT、Geminiその他もろもろ全部せ〜せい𐀁𑀡なんだけど文章生成は気にせず使う人多いなという印象、なぜ？？

The protest? Grok (SpaceX's AI) allegedly generating stuff it definitely shouldn't.
The response? This balloon with a "ketamine" tattoo and an unhinged grin.

Elon called it "too stupid."
I call it the funniest warning label Wall Street's ever ignored. 💊🤡

The crowd roars.

That roar wasn't politics.
It was humanity refusing to look away.

We need more of this energy everywhere.
